title: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - Successful UE Context Setup and Modification
objective: Verify that the inter-gNB-DU LTM procedure completes successfully with proper UE CONTEXT SETUP and MODIFICATION messages exchanged between gNB-CU, source gNB-DU, candidate gNB-DU, and UE.
preconditions: UE is connected to source gNB-DU under the same gNB-CU
UE performs L3 measurement and reports neighboring cells
Candidate gNB-DU is available and ready to accept UE CONTEXT SETUP
steps: UE sends MeasurementReport message to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU forwards MeasurementReport to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER
gNB-CU decides to initiate LTM configuration
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to candidate gNB-DU with candidate cell ID and CSI resource configuration
Candidate gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE including lower layer RRC configurations
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU with early sync info and LTM configuration ID mapping list
Source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE including updated lower layer configuration
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to candidate gNB-DU for subsequent LTM or configuration updates
Candidate gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE including updated lower layer configuration
expected_results: MeasurementReport is correctly forwarded from UE to gNB-CU
Candidate gNB-DU accepts UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST and returns valid UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE
Source gNB-DU accepts UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST and returns valid UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE
Candidate gNB-DU accepts subsequent UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST and returns valid UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE

title: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - RRC Reconfiguration and UE Response
objective: Verify that RRC Reconfiguration messages are properly transferred from gNB-CU to UE via source gNB-DU and that UE responds with RRCReconfigurationComplete.
preconditions: UE CONTEXT SETUP and MODIFICATION procedures have been completed successfully
gNB-CU has generated RRCReconfiguration message with LTM configuration
steps: gNB-CU sends DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER containing RRCReconfiguration to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfiguration message to UE
UE processes RRCReconfiguration and sends RRCReconfigurationComplete to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER
expected_results: UE receives and applies RRCReconfiguration message
UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message to source gNB-DU
RRCReconfigurationComplete is correctly forwarded to gNB-CU

title: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - Early TA Acquisition and Timing Advance Information Transfer
objective: Verify early Timing Advance (TA) acquisition and proper transfer of TA information between candidate gNB-DU, gNB-CU, and source gNB-DU.
preconditions: RRCReconfigurationComplete message has been exchanged
CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST is triggered based on L3 measurement
steps: gNB-CU sends CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST to candidate gNB-DU
Candidate gNB-DU performs early TA acquisition with UE
Candidate gNB-DU sends DU-CU TA INFORMATION TRANSFER message to gNB-CU including TA values and PRACH resource info
gNB-CU forwards CU-DU TA INFORMATION TRANSFER message to source gNB-DU with TA values and PRACH resource info
expected_results: Candidate gNB-DU successfully acquires early TA from UE
TA information and PRACH resource info are correctly transferred from candidate gNB-DU to gNB-CU
gNB-CU forwards TA information correctly to source gNB-DU

title: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - Cell Switch Command and Notifications
objective: Verify that the Cell Switch Command is sent to UE and that DU-CU and CU-DU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION messages are exchanged correctly between source gNB-DU, gNB-CU, and candidate gNB-DU.
preconditions: Early TA acquisition and timing advance information transfer completed
L1 measurement report received and LTM cell switch decision made
steps: Source gNB-DU sends Cell Switch Command to UE
Source gNB-DU sends DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION to gNB-CU with target cell ID and TCI state ID(s)
gNB-CU forwards CU-DU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION to candidate gNB-DU with target cell ID, TCI state ID(s), and TA info
expected_results: UE receives and processes Cell Switch Command
DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION is correctly sent from source gNB-DU to gNB-CU
CU-DU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION is correctly forwarded from gNB-CU to candidate gNB-DU

title: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - UE Access Detection and Access Success
objective: Verify that candidate gNB-DU detects UE access and sends ACCESS SUCCESS message to gNB-CU after cell switch command execution.
preconditions: Candidate gNB-DU received CU-DU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION
UE received and processed Cell Switch Command
steps: Candidate gNB-DU detects UE access as per TS 38.300
Candidate gNB-DU sends ACCESS SUCCESS message to gNB-CU with target cell ID
expected_results: Candidate gNB-DU successfully detects UE access
ACCESS SUCCESS message is correctly sent to gNB-CU with accurate target cell ID

title: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - UE RRC Reconfiguration Complete on Target Cell
objective: Verify that UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message to candidate gNB-DU after cell switch and that it is forwarded to gNB-CU.
preconditions: Candidate gNB-DU detected UE access and sent ACCESS SUCCESS
UE has switched to target cell
steps: UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message to candidate gNB-DU
Candidate gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER
expected_results: UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message successfully
Candidate gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete message correctly to gNB-CU

title: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - UE Context Release on Source gNB-DU
objective: Verify that after successful cell switch, gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND to source gNB-DU and source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE.
preconditions: UE has successfully switched to target gNB-DU and RRCReconfigurationComplete has been forwarded to gNB-CU
steps: gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND to source gNB-DU to release prepared cells
Source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE message
expected_results: UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND is correctly received by source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU releases resources and sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE message

title: F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP during Intra-NR Mobility
objective: Verify that the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP procedure is correctly performed when a UE moves within the same NR cell or between NR cells under the same gNB-DU
preconditions: UE is registered and connected to the source gNB-DU
UE initiates intra-NR mobility (handover) to a target cell within the same gNB-DU
F1AP interface between gNB-CU and gNB-DU is established and operational
steps: Trigger intra-NR mobility event for the UE
gNB-CU sends F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to the target gNB-DU
Target gNB-DU processes the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST
Target gNB-DU responds with F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE
gNB-CU completes the UE CONTEXT SETUP procedure
Verify UE context is established on the target gNB-DU
expected_results: F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST is correctly formatted and sent by gNB-CU
Target gNB-DU successfully processes the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST
F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE is received by gNB-CU without errors
UE context is correctly established on the target gNB-DU
No protocol errors or message retransmissions occur during the procedure

title: LTM Procedure with gNB-CU-UP Change - Successful Bearer Context Setup
objective: Verify that the LTM procedure with gNB-CU-UP change completes successfully including bearer context setup, RRC reconfiguration, and path switch.
preconditions: UE is connected to source gNB-DU and source gNB-CU-UP
Measurement report is available at source gNB-DU
Target gNB-DU and target gNB-CU-UP are reachable and configured
AMF and UPF are operational
steps: Source gNB-DU forwards Measurement Report to gNB-CU-CP
gNB-CU-CP decides to initiate LTM configuration
gNB-CU-CP sends BEARER CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to target gNB-CU-UP with UL TNL address for NG-U
Target gNB-CU-UP responds with BEARER CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE including UL TNL for F1-U, DL TNL for NG-U, and data forwarding TNL addresses
Perform LTM configuration procedures between gNB-CU and candidate gNB-DUs and source gNB-DU
gNB-CU-CP sends RRC Reconfiguration message to source gNB-DU via DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER
Source gNB-DU sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER to gNB-CU-CP
If LTM triggered by L3 measurement, gNB-CU-CP sends CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST to gNB-DU
UE sends lower layer measurement result to source gNB-DU if L1 measurement configured
Source gNB-DU decides to execute LTM to target cell
Source gNB-DU sends DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION with target cell ID to gNB-CU-CP
gNB-CU-CP forwards CU-DU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION with target cell ID to target gNB-DU
gNB-CU-CP sends BEARER CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-CU-UP to stop packet delivery and exchange PDCP UL/DL status and TNL info
Source gNB-CU-UP responds with BEARER CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE
gNB-CU-CP sends BEARER CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to target gNB-CU-UP with DL TNL info and PDCP UL/DL status
Target gNB-CU-UP responds with BEARER CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE
Data forwarding occurs from source gNB-CU-UP to target gNB-CU-UP
Target gNB-DU detects UE in target cell
Target gNB-DU sends ACCESS SUCCESS message to gNB-CU-CP and Downlink Data Delivery Status to target gNB-CU-UP
Path Switch procedure is performed to update DL TNL address towards core network
Bearer Context Release procedure is performed to release UE context in source gNB-CU-UP
expected_results: Measurement Report is successfully forwarded to gNB-CU-CP
LTM configuration decision is made and bearer context setup request is sent
Target gNB-CU-UP responds with correct bearer context setup response including all required TNL addresses
LTM configuration procedures complete without errors
RRC Reconfiguration and ReconfigurationComplete messages are exchanged successfully
CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST is sent if L3 measurement triggered LTM
Source gNB-DU correctly decides and initiates LTM to target cell
DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION messages are exchanged correctly with target cell ID
Bearer Context Modification procedures complete successfully with correct PDCP status and TNL address exchange
Data forwarding from source to target gNB-CU-UP is performed without packet loss
Target gNB-DU detects UE and sends ACCESS SUCCESS and Downlink Data Delivery Status
Path Switch procedure updates DL TNL address towards core network successfully
Bearer Context Release procedure releases UE context in source gNB-CU-UP without issues

title: LTM Procedure with gNB-CU-UP Change - CU-DU Mobility Initiation Request Handling
objective: Verify that the CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST message is correctly sent and handled when LTM is triggered based on L3 measurement.
preconditions: LTM is triggered based on L3 measurement
Source gNB-DU, gNB-CU-CP, and gNB-DU are operational
UE is connected and measurement reports are available
steps: Source gNB-DU forwards Measurement Report to gNB-CU-CP
gNB-CU-CP decides to initiate LTM configuration
gNB-CU-CP sends BEARER CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to target gNB-CU-UP
Target gNB-CU-UP responds with BEARER CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE
LTM configuration procedures are performed
gNB-CU-CP sends RRC Reconfiguration message to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message
gNB-CU-CP sends CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST message to gNB-DU
Verify that gNB-DU triggers early synchronization and/or cell switch upon receiving CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST
expected_results: CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST message is sent by gNB-CU-CP after RRC ReconfigurationComplete
gNB-DU receives CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST and triggers early synchronization and/or cell switch
No errors or delays occur in handling CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST

title: Bearer Context Modification Procedure During LTM with gNB-CU-UP Change
objective: Verify that Bearer Context Modification procedures between gNB-CU-CP and source/target gNB-CU-UP are correctly performed including PDCP UL/DL status exchange and TNL address updates.
preconditions: LTM procedure is ongoing with gNB-CU-UP change
Source and target gNB-CU-UP are reachable
Bearer contexts are established
steps: gNB-CU-CP sends BEARER CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-CU-UP to stop packet delivery and exchange PDCP UL/DL status and TNL info
Source gNB-CU-UP responds with BEARER CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE
gNB-CU-CP sends BEARER CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to target gNB-CU-UP with DL TNL address and PDCP UL/DL status
Target gNB-CU-UP responds with BEARER CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE
expected_results: Bearer Context Modification Request messages contain correct PDCP UL/DL status and TNL address information
Source gNB-CU-UP stops packet delivery upon request
Target gNB-CU-UP updates bearer context with new DL TNL address and PDCP status
Modification responses are received without errors

title: Data Forwarding Verification During LTM with gNB-CU-UP Change
objective: Verify that data forwarding from source gNB-CU-UP to target gNB-CU-UP is correctly performed during LTM procedure.
preconditions: Bearer Context Modification procedures completed successfully
Source and target gNB-CU-UP are connected
UE context is active
steps: Initiate data forwarding from source gNB-CU-UP to target gNB-CU-UP
Monitor data packets forwarded during LTM
Verify integrity and sequence of forwarded data
expected_results: Data forwarding occurs without packet loss or duplication
Forwarded data maintains integrity and correct sequence
No impact on UE data session continuity

title: Path Switch and Bearer Context Release After LTM with gNB-CU-UP Change
objective: Verify that path switch procedure updates DL TNL address towards core network and bearer context release procedure releases UE context in source gNB-CU-UP after LTM.
preconditions: LTM procedure with gNB-CU-UP change completed
Target gNB-DU has detected UE and sent ACCESS SUCCESS
Data forwarding is completed
steps: Perform Path Switch procedure to update DL TNL address for NG-U towards core network
Perform Bearer Context Release procedure to release UE context in source gNB-CU-UP
Verify signaling messages and responses during these procedures
expected_results: Path Switch procedure successfully updates DL TNL address in core network
Bearer Context Release procedure completes and UE context is removed from source gNB-CU-UP
No residual data or signaling errors remain after release

title: Intra-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - Successful UE Context Setup and Cell Switch
objective: Verify the correct execution of the intra-gNB-DU LTM procedure including UE context modification, RRC reconfiguration, early TA acquisition, cell switch command, and resource release.
preconditions: UE is connected and camped on a source cell within the same gNB-DU.
gNB-CU and gNB-DU are properly connected and synchronized.
L1 and L3 measurements are configured and enabled on the UE.
steps: UE sends MeasurementReport message with L3 measurement results to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards MeasurementReport to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
gNB-CU decides to initiate LTM configuration.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to gNB-DU with candidate cell ID and CSI resource configuration.
gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE including lower layer RRC configurations.
gNB-CU sends another UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST with updated CSI resource configuration and/or LTM configuration ID mapping list.
gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE with updated lower layer configuration.
gNB-CU sends DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER containing RRCReconfiguration message to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfiguration message to UE.
UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
gNB-CU sends CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST to gNB-DU to trigger early synchronization and/or cell switch.
UE performs early TA acquisition to candidate cell(s).
UE sends L1 measurement report to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU decides to execute LTM and sends Cell Switch Command to UE.
gNB-DU sends DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION with target cell ID to gNB-CU.
gNB-DU detects UE access in target cell.
gNB-DU sends ACCESS SUCCESS message with target cell ID to gNB-CU.
UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to gNB-DU to release resources of prepared cells.
gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE.
expected_results: MeasurementReport is successfully received and forwarded by gNB-DU and gNB-CU.
UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST and RESPONSE messages are exchanged correctly with valid configurations.
RRCReconfiguration message is correctly delivered to UE and RRCReconfigurationComplete is received and forwarded.
CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST triggers early TA acquisition successfully.
Cell Switch Command is correctly received and executed by UE.
DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION and ACCESS SUCCESS messages are correctly exchanged with valid target cell ID.
UE successfully accesses the target cell and completes RRC reconfiguration.
Resources of prepared cells are released properly after cell switch.
No message loss or protocol errors occur during the procedure.

title: Intra-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - Handling Multiple Candidate Cells
objective: Verify that the intra-gNB-DU LTM procedure correctly handles multiple candidate cells by performing multiple UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST and RESPONSE exchanges.
preconditions: UE is connected and camped on a source cell within the same gNB-DU.
gNB-CU and gNB-DU are properly connected and synchronized.
Multiple candidate cells are available within the same gNB-DU for LTM.
L1 and L3 measurements are configured and enabled on the UE.
steps: UE sends MeasurementReport message with L3 measurement results to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards MeasurementReport to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
gNB-CU decides to initiate LTM configuration for multiple candidate cells.
For each candidate cell, gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to gNB-DU with candidate cell ID and CSI resource configuration.
gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE including lower layer RRC configurations for each candidate cell.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST with updated CSI resource configuration and/or LTM configuration ID mapping list.
gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE with updated lower layer configuration.
gNB-CU sends DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER containing RRCReconfiguration message to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfiguration message to UE.
UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
gNB-CU sends CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST to gNB-DU to trigger early synchronization and/or cell switch.
UE performs early TA acquisition to candidate cell(s).
UE sends L1 measurement report to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU decides to execute LTM and sends Cell Switch Command to UE.
gNB-DU sends DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION with target cell ID to gNB-CU.
gNB-DU detects UE access in target cell.
gNB-DU sends ACCESS SUCCESS message with target cell ID to gNB-CU.
UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to gNB-DU to release resources of prepared cells.
gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE.
expected_results: Multiple UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST and RESPONSE messages are correctly exchanged for each candidate cell.
Lower layer RRC configurations for all candidate cells are correctly generated and accepted.
RRCReconfiguration and RRCReconfigurationComplete messages are correctly exchanged.
Early TA acquisition and cell switch procedures are successfully completed for the selected target cell.
Resources for non-selected candidate cells are properly released after cell switch.
No message loss or protocol errors occur during multiple candidate cell handling.

title: Intra-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - Negative Test: UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE Rejection
objective: Verify system behavior when gNB-DU rejects the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST during LTM configuration.
preconditions: UE is connected and camped on a source cell within the same gNB-DU.
gNB-CU and gNB-DU are properly connected and synchronized.
L1 and L3 measurements are configured and enabled on the UE.
gNB-DU is configured to reject UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST for testing.
steps: UE sends MeasurementReport message with L3 measurement results to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards MeasurementReport to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
gNB-CU decides to initiate LTM configuration.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to gNB-DU with candidate cell ID and CSI resource configuration.
gNB-DU rejects the request and sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE indicating rejection or failure.
gNB-CU handles the rejection accordingly and does not proceed with further LTM configuration.
Verify that no RRCReconfiguration message is sent to UE.
Verify that no cell switch command is issued.
expected_results: gNB-DU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE indicating rejection or failure.
gNB-CU correctly handles the rejection and aborts LTM configuration.
No RRCReconfiguration or cell switch commands are sent to UE.
UE remains camped on the source cell without interruption.
No protocol errors or crashes occur in gNB-CU or gNB-DU.

title: Intra-gNB-DU LTM Procedure - Early TA Acquisition Failure Handling
objective: Verify system behavior when UE fails early TA acquisition during intra-gNB-DU LTM procedure.
preconditions: UE is connected and camped on a source cell within the same gNB-DU.
gNB-CU and gNB-DU are properly connected and synchronized.
L1 and L3 measurements are configured and enabled on the UE.
UE is configured or forced to fail early TA acquisition.
steps: Complete steps 1 to 10a as per normal intra-gNB-DU LTM procedure.
UE attempts early TA acquisition to candidate cell(s) but fails.
UE sends L1 measurement report indicating failure or no valid measurements.
gNB-DU decides on LTM execution based on failed TA acquisition.
Verify if gNB-DU aborts LTM procedure or retries early TA acquisition.
Verify that no Cell Switch Command is sent to UE if TA acquisition fails.
Verify system stability and proper error handling.
expected_results: UE reports failure or invalid L1 measurements to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU either aborts LTM procedure or triggers retry mechanisms as per implementation.
No Cell Switch Command is sent to UE if early TA acquisition fails.
System logs appropriate error or warning messages.
No protocol deadlocks or crashes occur.
UE remains connected on the source cell without disruption.

title: Inter-gNB-DU Conditional Handover UE Context Setup Request and Response
objective: Verify that the gNB-CU correctly sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to candidate gNB-DU and receives UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE with requested target cell ID during conditional handover or conditional PSCell change.
preconditions: UE is connected to source gNB-DU under the same gNB-CU
UE has sent MeasurementReport to gNB-CU indicating candidate cells
Conditional handover or conditional PSCell change conditions are met
steps: gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to candidate gNB-DU including HandoverPreparationInformation or CG-ConfigInfo
Candidate gNB-DU processes the request and responds with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including requested target cell ID
expected_results: Candidate gNB-DU successfully creates UE context and data bearers as requested
UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message contains the correct target cell ID
No errors or rejections in UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE

title: DL and UL RRC Message Transfer for Conditional Handover Execution
objective: Validate the DL and UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER procedures between gNB-CU, source gNB-DU, and UE during conditional handover or PSCell change.
preconditions: UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE has been received by gNB-CU
UE is connected to source gNB-DU
steps: gNB-CU sends DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER containing RRCReconfiguration to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfiguration to UE
UE applies RRCReconfiguration and sends RRCReconfigurationComplete to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER
expected_results: UE successfully applies RRCReconfiguration
RRCReconfigurationComplete messages are correctly forwarded without loss
No protocol errors during RRC message transfer

title: Conditional Handover Execution and Random Access Procedure at Candidate gNB-DU
objective: Ensure that upon fulfillment of execution conditions, UE performs Random Access at candidate gNB-DU and target gNB-DU informs gNB-CU of access success and downlink data delivery status.
preconditions: RRCReconfigurationComplete has been received by gNB-CU
Execution condition for conditional handover or PSCell change is fulfilled
steps: UE initiates Random Access procedure at candidate gNB-DU
Candidate gNB-DU processes Random Access and upon success becomes target gNB-DU
Target gNB-DU sends Downlink Data Delivery Status frame to gNB-CU
Target gNB-DU sends ACCESS SUCCESS message to gNB-CU indicating the accessed cell
expected_results: Random Access procedure completes successfully
Downlink Data Delivery Status frame is correctly sent and received by gNB-CU
ACCESS SUCCESS message correctly indicates the target cell
No data loss or protocol errors during access

title: UE Context Modification Request and Response after Conditional Handover
objective: Verify that gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU to stop data transmission and source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE.
preconditions: UE has successfully accessed target gNB-DU
Downlink Data Delivery Status and ACCESS SUCCESS messages have been exchanged
steps: gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU indicating to stop data transmission
Source gNB-DU sends Downlink Data Delivery Status frame to gNB-CU about unsuccessfully transmitted downlink data
gNB-CU forwards downlink packets including PDCP PDUs not successfully transmitted to target gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE to gNB-CU
expected_results: Source gNB-DU stops data transmission as requested
Downlink Data Delivery Status frame correctly informs gNB-CU
Unsuccessfully transmitted PDCP PDUs are forwarded to target gNB-DU
UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE is received without errors

title: UE Context Release Procedure Post Conditional Handover
objective: Confirm that gNB-CU initiates UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND and receives UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE from source gNB-DU when applicable after conditional handover or PSCell change.
preconditions: UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE has been received from source gNB-DU
Conditional handover or PSCell change has been successfully executed
Not all PSCells in source gNB-DU remain configured as candidate PSCells in subsequent CPAC
steps: gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU releases UE context and sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE to gNB-CU
expected_results: UE context is successfully released at source gNB-DU
UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE message is received by gNB-CU
No residual data transmission or signaling related to released UE context

title: Intra-gNB-DU Handover with UL and DL GTP TEID Update
objective: Verify that during intra-gNB-DU handover, the gNB-CU and gNB-DU correctly update UL and DL GTP TEIDs and handle PDCP PDU transmission accordingly.
preconditions: UE is connected to a cell served by gNB-DU and gNB-CU.
UE supports intra-gNB-DU handover and UE Context Modification procedure as per TS 38.473.
Initial UL and DL GTP TEIDs are established between gNB-CU and gNB-DU.
steps: Trigger intra-gNB-DU handover (inter-cell or intra-cell) for the UE.
gNB-CU provides new UL GTP TEID to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU provides new DL GTP TEID to gNB-CU.
gNB-DU continues sending UL PDCP PDUs to gNB-CU using previous UL GTP TEID until RLC is re-established.
After RLC re-establishment, gNB-DU starts sending UL PDCP PDUs using new UL GTP TEID.
gNB-CU continues sending DL PDCP PDUs to gNB-DU using previous DL GTP TEID until PDCP re-establishment or PDCP data recovery is performed.
After PDCP re-establishment or data recovery, gNB-CU starts sending DL PDCP PDUs using new DL GTP TEID.
expected_results: gNB-DU receives and applies new UL GTP TEID from gNB-CU.
gNB-CU receives and applies new DL GTP TEID from gNB-DU.
UL PDCP PDUs are transmitted using previous UL GTP TEID until RLC re-establishment.
UL PDCP PDUs are transmitted using new UL GTP TEID after RLC re-establishment.
DL PDCP PDUs are transmitted using previous DL GTP TEID until PDCP re-establishment or data recovery.
DL PDCP PDUs are transmitted using new DL GTP TEID after PDCP re-establishment or data recovery.
No loss or duplication of PDCP PDUs during the handover procedure.

title: Inter-gNB-DU Mobility - Successful UE Context Setup and Handover
objective: Verify that the UE context is successfully setup at the target gNB-DU and the UE performs handover within the same gNB-CU.
preconditions: UE is connected to source gNB-DU under the same gNB-CU
UE is in connected mode and capable of sending MeasurementReport
gNB-CU, source gNB-DU and target gNB-DU are properly configured and reachable
steps: UE sends MeasurementReport to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU forwards MeasurementReport to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER
gNB-CU optionally sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU to query latest config
Source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE including full configuration
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to target gNB-DU with HandoverPreparationInformation
Target gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST with RRCReconfiguration to source gNB-DU and indicates to stop data transmission
Source gNB-DU sends Downlink Data Delivery Status frame to gNB-CU
Source gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfiguration message to UE
Source gNB-DU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE to gNB-CU
UE performs Random Access procedure at target gNB-DU
Target gNB-DU sends Downlink Data Delivery Status frame to gNB-CU
UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete to target gNB-DU
Target gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete to gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU releases UE context and sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE to gNB-CU
expected_results: MeasurementReport is successfully received and forwarded by source gNB-DU and gNB-CU
UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST/RESPONSE exchange completes successfully
UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST is accepted by target gNB-DU with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE
RRCReconfiguration message is correctly delivered to UE via source gNB-DU
Downlink Data Delivery Status frames are correctly sent to gNB-CU from source and target gNB-DUs
Random Access procedure at target gNB-DU completes successfully
UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete and it is forwarded to gNB-CU
UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND and RELEASE COMPLETE messages are exchanged successfully
UE context is correctly released at source gNB-DU
UE successfully hands over from source gNB-DU to target gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU

title: Inter-gNB-DU Mobility - UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST without stopping data transmission (DAPS Handover scenario)
objective: Verify that during DAPS handover, UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST may not indicate stopping data transmission and handover command is delivered via DL RRC Message Transfer.
preconditions: UE is connected to source gNB-DU under the same gNB-CU
DAPS handover is configured and supported by gNB-CU and gNB-DUs
UE is in connected mode and capable of sending MeasurementReport
steps: UE sends MeasurementReport to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU forwards MeasurementReport to gNB-CU
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to target gNB-DU with HandoverPreparationInformation
Target gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU without indication to stop data transmission or only for DRBs not subject to DAPS handover
Handover command is sent to UE via DL RRC Message Transfer procedure
Source gNB-DU sends Downlink Data Delivery Status frame to gNB-CU after UE accesses target gNB-DU
UE performs Random Access procedure at target gNB-DU
UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete to target gNB-DU
Target gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete to gNB-CU
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU releases UE context and sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE
expected_results: UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST does not stop data transmission for DRBs subject to DAPS handover
Handover command is successfully delivered to UE via DL RRC Message Transfer
Downlink Data Delivery Status frames are correctly sent after UE accesses target gNB-DU
Random Access procedure completes successfully at target gNB-DU
RRCReconfigurationComplete message is forwarded correctly
UE context is released at source gNB-DU after successful handover

title: Inter-gNB-DU Mobility - UE Context Setup Failure at Target gNB-DU
objective: Verify system behavior when UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST is rejected or fails at target gNB-DU.
preconditions: UE is connected to source gNB-DU under the same gNB-CU
Target gNB-DU is reachable but unable to setup UE context (e.g. resource constraints)
steps: UE sends MeasurementReport to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU forwards MeasurementReport to gNB-CU
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to target gNB-DU
Target gNB-DU rejects or fails to respond with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE
gNB-CU handles failure accordingly (e.g. abort handover, notify source gNB-DU and UE)
expected_results: UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST is sent to target gNB-DU
No UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE or negative response is received
gNB-CU detects failure and does not proceed with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU
UE remains connected to source gNB-DU without interruption
Appropriate error handling or recovery procedures are triggered

title: Inter-gNB-DU Mobility - UE Context Release at Source gNB-DU after Successful Handover
objective: Verify that UE context is properly released at source gNB-DU after successful handover to target gNB-DU.
preconditions: UE has successfully completed handover to target gNB-DU
gNB-CU is aware of successful UE access at target gNB-DU
steps: gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND to source gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU releases UE context
Source gNB-DU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE message to gNB-CU
expected_results: UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND is received and processed by source gNB-DU
UE context is cleared and resources are freed at source gNB-DU
UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE message is sent to gNB-CU confirming release

title: Verify RLF Report signalling from UE to gNB-CU via RRC UE Information Request/Response
objective: Ensure that a UE with a logged RLF Report correctly sends the RLF Report to the connected gNB-CU using the RRC UE Information Request/Response procedure.
preconditions: UE has a logged RLF Report
UE is connected to a cell served by gNB-CU (gNB2 in example)
steps: Trigger UE to initiate RRC UE Information Request/Response procedure to send RLF Report
Monitor RRC messages exchanged between UE and gNB-CU
expected_results: UE sends RLF Report within RRC UE Information Request/Response message to gNB-CU
gNB-CU successfully receives and processes the RLF Report

title: Verify Xn: Failure Indication message carrying RLF Report from gNB-CU to previous gNB-CU
objective: Validate that the gNB-CU receiving the RLF Report from the UE sends an Xn: Failure Indication message including the RLF Report to the previous gNB-CU where the UE was connected before failure.
preconditions: gNB-CU (gNB2) has received RLF Report from UE
Previous gNB-CU (gNB1-CU) is reachable via Xn interface
steps: gNB-CU sends Xn: Failure Indication message to previous gNB-CU
Monitor Xn interface messages between gNB-CU and previous gNB-CU
expected_results: Xn: Failure Indication message includes the RLF Report
Previous gNB-CU receives and processes the Failure Indication with RLF Report

title: Verify F1: Access and Mobility Indication message carrying RLF Report from gNB-CU to gNB-DU
objective: Ensure that the gNB-CU sends the F1: Access and Mobility Indication message including the RLF Report to the gNB-DU.
preconditions: gNB-CU has received RLF Report from UE or from Xn: Failure Indication
gNB-DU is connected and reachable via F1 interface
steps: gNB-CU sends F1: Access and Mobility Indication message to gNB-DU including RLF Report
Monitor F1 interface messages between gNB-CU and gNB-DU
expected_results: F1: Access and Mobility Indication message contains the RLF Report
gNB-DU receives and processes the RLF Report correctly

title: Verify direct signalling of RLF Report from gNB-CU to gNB-DU via F1: Access and Mobility Indication
objective: Validate that gNB-CU can directly signal the RLF Report received from UE to gNB-DU using the F1: Access and Mobility Indication procedure without intermediate Xn signalling.
preconditions: gNB-CU has received RLF Report from UE
gNB-DU is connected and reachable via F1 interface
steps: gNB-CU sends F1: Access and Mobility Indication message including RLF Report directly to gNB-DU
Monitor F1 interface messages
expected_results: F1: Access and Mobility Indication message includes RLF Report
gNB-DU receives and processes the RLF Report

title: Verify UE Context Setup during Intra-gNB-CU Mobility
objective: Ensure that the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP procedure is correctly performed when a UE moves within the same gNB-CU
preconditions: UE is registered and connected to the source gNB-CU
Source gNB-CU and target gNB-CU are part of the same physical gNB infrastructure
F1 interface between gNB-CU and gNB-DU is established and operational
steps: Trigger UE mobility event within the same gNB-CU (Intra-gNB-CU mobility)
Source gNB-CU initiates UE CONTEXT SETUP procedure towards the target gNB-CU
Send F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message with necessary UE context information
Target gNB-CU processes the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST and allocates required resources
Target gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message back to source gNB-CU
Source gNB-CU confirms successful UE CONTEXT SETUP and updates UE context accordingly
expected_results: UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST is correctly formatted and contains all mandatory IE fields
Target gNB-CU successfully processes the request and allocates resources without errors
UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE is received by source gNB-CU confirming successful setup
UE context is updated correctly in both source and target gNB-CU
No message loss or protocol errors occur during the procedure

title: F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP Procedure for EN-DC Mobility
objective: Verify that the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP procedure is correctly performed during EN-DC mobility scenarios to establish UE context on the target gNB.
preconditions: UE is connected to a source gNB with EN-DC configured.
Source gNB and target gNB are connected via F1 interface.
UE is in connected mode with active data sessions.
steps: Trigger EN-DC mobility event requiring UE context setup on target gNB.
Source gNB sends F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to target gNB.
Target gNB processes the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST and allocates necessary resources.
Target gNB sends F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message back to source gNB.
Source gNB forwards necessary signaling to UE to complete mobility.
expected_results: Target gNB successfully receives and processes UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST.
Target gNB allocates resources and establishes UE context without errors.
UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE is sent successfully to source gNB.
UE continues data session seamlessly after mobility.
No protocol errors or message retransmissions occur during the procedure.

title: Intra-CU Topology Adaptation Procedure in Standalone (SA) Mode
objective: Verify correct execution of the intra-CU topology adaptation procedure for a migrating IAB-node within the same IAB-donor-CU in SA mode, including UE context setup, modification, and release between source and target parent IAB-DUs.
preconditions: Migrating IAB-MT is connected to source parent IAB-DU under the same IAB-donor-CU.
Measurement Configuration is provided to migrating IAB-MT by IAB-donor-CU.
Source and target parent IAB-DUs may use different IAB-donor-DUs.
F1AP and RRC protocols are operational between involved nodes.
steps: Migrating IAB-MT sends MeasurementReport to source parent IAB-DU.
Source parent IAB-DU forwards MeasurementReport to IAB-donor-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
IAB-donor-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to target parent IAB-DU.
Target parent IAB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE.
IAB-donor-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST with RRCReconfiguration message to source parent IAB-DU, including BH RLC channel and BAP Routing ID configuration for target path.
Source parent IAB-DU forwards RRCReconfiguration to migrating IAB-MT.
Source parent IAB-DU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE to IAB-donor-CU.
Migrating IAB-MT performs Random Access procedure at target parent IAB-DU.
Migrating IAB-MT sends RRCReconfigurationComplete to target parent IAB-DU.
Target parent IAB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete to IAB-donor-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
IAB-donor-CU configures BH RLC channels and BAP routing on target path and updates F1-U tunnels with new TNL addresses.
IAB-donor-CU switches migrating IAB-node's F1 association to new TNL addresses and updates UL BH information.
IAB-donor-CU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND to source parent IAB-DU.
Source parent IAB-DU releases migrating IAB-MT context and sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE to IAB-donor-CU.
IAB-donor-CU releases BH RLC channels and BAP routing entries on source path.
expected_results: MeasurementReport is successfully received and forwarded to IAB-donor-CU.
UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST and RESPONSE messages are exchanged successfully between IAB-donor-CU and target parent IAB-DU.
UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST with correct RRCReconfiguration is delivered to migrating IAB-MT via source parent IAB-DU.
Random Access procedure at target parent IAB-DU completes successfully.
RRCReconfigurationComplete is received and forwarded to IAB-donor-CU.
BH RLC channels and BAP routing entries are correctly configured on target path.
F1-C and F1-U tunnels are switched to new TNL addresses without data loss or race conditions.
UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND and COMPLETE messages are exchanged successfully between IAB-donor-CU and source parent IAB-DU.
BH RLC channels and BAP routing entries on source path are released properly, except for common nodes if applicable.

title: Intra-CU Topology Adaptation Procedure in Non-Standalone (NSA) Mode Using MCG SRB
objective: Validate the intra-CU topology adaptation procedure for a migrating IAB-node using MCG SRB during EN-DC operation within the same IAB-donor-CU, including context setup, modification, SGNB modification, and release.
preconditions: Migrating IAB-MT is connected to source parent IAB-DU under the same IAB-donor-CU with only MCG SRB available.
EN-DC operation is active with MeNB and IAB-donor-CU.
Source and target parent IAB-DUs may use different IAB-donor-DUs.
F1AP and RRC protocols are operational between involved nodes.
steps: Migrating IAB-MT sends ULInformationTransferMRDC message containing NR RRC MeasurementReport to MeNB.
MeNB forwards RRC TRANSFER message to IAB-donor-CU.
IAB-donor-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source parent IAB-DU to query SCG configuration.
Source parent IAB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE including full configuration.
IAB-donor-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST with CG-ConfigInfo to target parent IAB-DU.
Target parent IAB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE.
IAB-donor-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST with Transmission Action Indicator to source parent IAB-DU and receives Downlink Data Delivery Status.
Source parent IAB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE.
IAB-donor-CU sends SGNB MODIFICATION REQUIRED to MeNB.
MeNB and SgNB perform SGNB MODIFICATION REQUEST and ACK procedures.
MeNB and migrating IAB-MT perform RRC Connection Reconfiguration procedure using MCG SRB.
MeNB sends SGNB MODIFICATION CONFIRM to IAB-donor-CU.
Migrating IAB-MT performs Random Access procedure at target parent IAB-DU.
IAB-donor-CU configures BH RLC channels, BAP routing, and redirects F1 association to new TNL addresses on target path.
IAB-donor-CU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND to source parent IAB-DU.
Source parent IAB-DU releases migrating IAB-MT context and sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE.
IAB-donor-CU releases BH RLC channels and BAP routing entries on source path.
expected_results: ULInformationTransferMRDC and RRC TRANSFER messages are successfully exchanged.
UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST and RESPONSE messages correctly query and provide SCG configuration.
UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST and RESPONSE messages are exchanged successfully between IAB-donor-CU and target parent IAB-DU.
Transmission Action Indicator correctly instructs source parent IAB-DU to stop data transmission.
Downlink Data Delivery Status is correctly reported to IAB-donor-CU.
SGNB MODIFICATION REQUIRED, REQUEST, ACK, and CONFIRM messages are successfully exchanged between IAB-donor-CU and MeNB.
RRC Connection Reconfiguration procedure completes successfully using MCG SRB.
Random Access procedure at target parent IAB-DU completes successfully.
BH RLC channels and BAP routing entries are correctly configured on target path.
F1-C and F1-U tunnels are switched to new TNL addresses without data loss or race conditions.
UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND and COMPLETE messages are exchanged successfully between IAB-donor-CU and source parent IAB-DU.
BH RLC channels and BAP routing entries on source path are released properly.

title: Intra-CU Backhaul RLF Recovery Procedure for IAB-node in SA Mode
objective: Verify that an IAB-node undergoing backhaul RLF recovery in SA mode successfully migrates to a new parent node under the same IAB-donor-CU and establishes RRC connection with updated TNL addresses and UL mappings.
preconditions: IAB-node is connected to an initial parent node served by an initial IAB-donor-DU under an IAB-donor-CU.
IAB-MT is configured to detect backhaul RLF as per TS 38.331 clause 5.3.10.3.
The network supports intra-CU topology adaptation procedure as per clause 8.2.3.1.
steps: Simulate backhaul RLF occurrence on the initial path for the IAB-MT.
IAB-MT declares BH RLF for the MCG according to TS 38.331 clause 5.3.10.3.
Trigger RRC re-establishment procedure at the new parent IAB-node DU as defined in clause 8.7.
Verify that the IAB-donor-CU provides new TNL address(es) anchored at the new IAB-donor-DU via RRC signalling.
Verify that the IAB-donor-CU provides new default UL mapping including default BH RLC channel and default BAP Routing ID for UL F1-C/non-F1 traffic via RRCReconfiguration message.
Execute steps 11-15 of the intra-CU topology adaptation procedure (clause 8.2.3.1) to complete recovery.
Verify that descendant nodes of the recovering IAB-node switch to new TNL address(es) and receive new default UL mapping via RRC.
expected_results: IAB-MT successfully declares BH RLF upon backhaul failure.
RRC re-establishment is successfully completed at the new parent IAB-node DU.
New TNL address(es) anchored at the new IAB-donor-DU are received and applied by the IAB-MT.
New default UL mapping including BH RLC channel and BAP Routing ID is received and applied.
Intra-CU topology adaptation steps complete without errors.
Descendant nodes switch to new TNL address(es) and apply new UL mappings as expected.
Downlink and uplink user data flow resumes correctly over the recovery path.

title: Establishment of Redundant Path in IAB Topology
objective: Verify the procedure for establishing a second redundant path in the IAB topology under the same IAB-donor-CU.
preconditions: IAB nodes operate in Standalone (SA) mode supporting NR-DC.
Dual-connecting IAB-node is connected to the first parent IAB-DU and IAB-donor-CU.
Measurement Configuration is provided to the dual-connecting IAB-MT by the IAB-donor-CU.
First and second parent IAB-DUs and IAB-donor-CU are operational.
steps: Dual-connecting IAB-MT sends MeasurementReport to the first parent IAB-DU.
First parent IAB-DU forwards MeasurementReport to IAB-donor-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
IAB-donor-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to the second parent IAB-DU to create UE context and set up bearers.
Second parent IAB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE to IAB-donor-CU.
IAB-donor-CU sends DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER with RRCReconfiguration (including TNL addresses if applicable) to first parent IAB-DU.
First parent IAB-DU forwards RRCReconfiguration to dual-connecting IAB-MT.
Dual-connecting IAB-MT sends RRCReconfigurationComplete to first parent IAB-DU.
First parent IAB-DU forwards RRCReconfigurationComplete to IAB-donor-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER.
Random Access procedure is performed at the second parent IAB-DU.
IAB-donor-CU configures BH RLC channels and BAP-layer route entries on the second path.
New TNL addresses are added to dual-connecting IAB-DU’s F1-C associations; F1AP gNB-DU CONFIGURATION UPDATE is sent if needed.
IAB-donor-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to migrate F1-U tunnels to the second path.
Dual-connecting IAB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE.
expected_results: MeasurementReport is successfully received and forwarded to IAB-donor-CU.
UE context and bearers are successfully created on the second parent IAB-DU.
RRCReconfiguration message with correct TNL addresses is delivered to dual-connecting IAB-MT.
RRCReconfigurationComplete is received and forwarded to IAB-donor-CU.
Random Access procedure completes successfully on the second parent IAB-DU.
BH RLC channels and BAP-layer route entries are configured correctly on the second path.
New TNL addresses are correctly added to F1-C associations and F1AP configuration update is sent if applicable.
F1-U tunnels are migrated to the second path as requested.
UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE confirms successful migration.

title: Redundant Path Establishment for Descendant IAB Nodes
objective: Verify that redundant path establishment procedure correctly configures descendant IAB-DUs when the second path uses a different IAB-donor-DU.
preconditions: Dual-connecting IAB-node has descendant IAB-DUs connected via the first path.
Second path uses a different IAB-donor-DU than the first path.
IAB-donor-CU and involved IAB-DUs are operational.
steps: IAB-donor-CU configures descendant IAB-DUs with new TNL addresses anchored on the second path IAB-donor-DU.
IAB-donor-CU configures BH RLC channels and BAP-layer route entries on the second path for descendant nodes.
New TNL addresses are added to descendant IAB-DUs’ F1-C associations.
IAB-donor-CU configures UL BH information for the second path to carry F1AP messages for descendant nodes.
IAB-donor-CU migrates F1-U tunnels for descendant nodes from first path to second path.
expected_results: Descendant IAB-DUs receive and apply new TNL addresses correctly.
BH RLC channels and BAP-layer route entries are configured properly on the second path for descendant nodes.
F1-C associations of descendant IAB-DUs are updated with new TNL addresses.
UL BH information is configured to support F1AP message transport on the second path.
F1-U tunnels for descendant nodes are successfully migrated to the second path.

title: Release of Redundant Path in IAB Topology
objective: Verify that the IAB-donor-CU can initiate and complete the release of a redundant path by releasing BAP routing entries and modifying/releasing BH RLC channels.
preconditions: Redundant path between dual-connecting IAB-node and second path IAB-donor-DU is established and active.
IAB-donor-CU and involved IAB-DUs are operational.
steps: IAB-donor-CU initiates release of the redundant path.
IAB-donor-CU releases BAP routing entries associated with the redundant path.
IAB-donor-CU modifies or releases BH RLC channels on the redundant path.
IAB-donor-CU updates F1-C associations to remove TNL addresses related to the redundant path if applicable.
expected_results: BAP routing entries for the redundant path are successfully released.
BH RLC channels on the redundant path are correctly modified or released.
F1-C associations are updated to reflect the removal of the redundant path.
Redundant path is no longer used for user or signaling traffic.

title: Verify UE Context Setup during Multicast Mobility Procedure
objective: To validate the correct setup of UE context on the F1 interface during a multicast mobility procedure in 5G network.
preconditions: UE is registered and connected to the source gNB.
Multicast session is active for the UE.
Source gNB and target gNB are connected via F1 interface.
steps: Trigger a multicast mobility event for the UE (e.g., UE moves from source gNB to target gNB).
Source gNB initiates UE Context Setup Request over F1AP to target gNB.
Target gNB processes the UE Context Setup Request and allocates necessary resources.
Target gNB sends UE Context Setup Response to source gNB.
Source gNB forwards multicast data to target gNB for the UE.
expected_results: UE Context Setup Request is correctly formatted and contains all necessary multicast context information.
Target gNB successfully allocates resources and responds with UE Context Setup Response.
Multicast data flow is uninterrupted and correctly forwarded to the UE via the target gNB.
No errors or protocol violations occur during the UE Context Setup procedure.

title: Inter-gNB-DU Mobility with SCG SRB (SRB3) UE CONTEXT SETUP
objective: Verify that the UE CONTEXT SETUP procedure correctly handles inter-gNB-DU mobility when SCG SRB (SRB3) is available during EN-DC operation, including the presence of CG-ConfigInfo in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message.
preconditions: UE is in EN-DC operation with SCG SRB (SRB3) established
UE is connected to source gNB-DU
Target gNB-DU is prepared to receive UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST
Inter-gNB-DU mobility scenario is triggered
steps: Trigger UE movement from source gNB-DU to target gNB-DU
Source gNB-DU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to target gNB-DU
Verify that UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message includes CG-ConfigInfo IE
Target gNB-DU processes UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST and responds accordingly
Verify that the procedure follows the inter-gNB-DU mobility procedure as defined in clause 8.2.1.1 with the addition of CG-ConfigInfo
expected_results: UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message is received by target gNB-DU with CG-ConfigInfo included
Target gNB-DU successfully processes the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message
Inter-gNB-DU mobility procedure completes without errors
UE context is correctly established on the target gNB-DU with SCG SRB (SRB3) maintained

title: Inter-gNB Handover with gNB-CU-UP Change - Basic Procedure
objective: Verify the inter-gNB handover procedure involving gNB-CU-UP change as per TS 37.340, including message exchanges and context setup.
preconditions: UE is attached and connected to source gNB-CU-CP and gNB-CU-UP via source gNB-DU.
Source and target gNB-CU-CP and gNB-CU-UP are reachable over F1 and Xn interfaces.
Bearer contexts are established on source gNB.
steps: Source gNB-CU-CP sends HANDOVER REQUEST to target gNB-CU-CP.
Target gNB-CU-CP performs Bearer Context Setup procedure (steps 2-4).
Target gNB-CU-CP sends HANDOVER REQUEST ACKNOWLEDGE to source gNB-CU-CP.
Source gNB-CU-CP performs F1 UE Context Modification procedure to send handover command to UE and indicate stop of data transmission.
Source gNB-CU-CP initiates Bearer Context Modification procedure to retrieve PDCP UL/DL status and exchange data forwarding info.
Source gNB-CU-CP sends SN STATUS TRANSFER message to target gNB-CU-CP.
Target gNB-CU-CP performs Bearer Context Modification procedure as per clause 8.9.2.
Data forwarding is performed from source gNB-CU-UP to target gNB-CU-UP.
Path Switch procedure is performed to update DL TNL address towards core network.
Target gNB-CU-CP sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE to source gNB-CU-CP.
Bearer Context Release and F1 UE Context Release procedures are performed on source gNB-DU.
expected_results: HANDOVER REQUEST and HANDOVER REQUEST ACKNOWLEDGE messages are successfully exchanged.
Bearer contexts are successfully setup on target gNB.
UE receives handover command and stops data transmission as indicated.
PDCP UL/DL status and data forwarding information are correctly exchanged.
SN STATUS TRANSFER message is correctly sent and processed.
Data forwarding from source to target gNB-CU-UP occurs without loss.
Path Switch updates DL TNL address correctly in NG-U towards core network.
UE context is released on source gNB-CU-CP and source gNB-DU.
Bearer contexts are released on source gNB.

title: Inter-gNB Conditional Handover with gNB-CU-UP Change
objective: Validate the inter-gNB handover procedure involving Conditional Handover (CHO) with gNB-CU-UP change, ensuring correct handling of EARLY STATUS TRANSFER and conditional resource release.
preconditions: UE is attached and connected to source gNB-CU-CP and gNB-CU-UP.
Target gNB is configured as candidate gNB for Conditional Handover.
F1 and Xn interfaces are established between source and target gNB components.
steps: Source gNB-CU-CP sends HANDOVER REQUEST to target gNB-CU-CP (target regarded as candidate gNB).
Target gNB-CU-CP performs Bearer Context Setup procedure.
Target gNB-CU-CP sends HANDOVER REQUEST ACKNOWLEDGE with data forwarding info.
Source gNB-CU-CP performs F1 UE Context Modification procedure without indicating stop of data transmission.
Bearer Context Modification procedure is performed to retrieve PDCP UL/DL status and exchange data forwarding info.
Source gNB-CU-CP sends EARLY STATUS TRANSFER message to target gNB-CU-CP.
Target gNB-CU-CP performs Bearer Context Modification procedure providing COUNT info to target gNB-CU-UP.
UE performs RACH upon fulfillment of CHO conditions and accesses target gNB-DU.
Target gNB-DU sends ACCESS SUCCESS message to target gNB-CU-CP.
Target gNB-CU-CP forwards necessary info to target gNB-CU-UP and releases resources on other candidate gNB-CU-UPs if any.
Target gNB-CU-CP sends HANDOVER SUCCESS to source gNB-CU-CP.
F1 UE Context Modification procedure is performed to indicate stop of data transmission.
Bearer Context Modification procedures are performed to stop packet delivery and retrieve PDCP UL/DL status.
SN STATUS TRANSFER message is sent from source to target gNB-CU-CP.
Bearer Context Modification procedure provides PDCP UL/DL status to target gNB-CU-UP if needed.
Inactivity monitoring is performed after bearer context modifications.
Path Switch procedure updates DL TNL address towards core network.
UE CONTEXT RELEASE and Bearer Context Release procedures are performed on source gNB.
expected_results: HANDOVER REQUEST and ACKNOWLEDGE messages are exchanged with candidate gNB.
EARLY STATUS TRANSFER message is correctly forwarded to appropriate gNB-CU-UP.
UE successfully performs RACH and accesses target gNB-DU.
ACCESS SUCCESS message is received and processed by target gNB-CU-CP.
Conditional handover resources on other candidate gNB-CU-UPs are released.
HANDOVER SUCCESS message is sent to source gNB-CU-CP.
Data transmission stop is correctly indicated after UE access.
PDCP UL/DL status is correctly exchanged and preserved if required.
Inactivity monitoring is triggered appropriately.
Path Switch updates DL TNL address correctly.
UE context and bearer contexts are released on source gNB.

title: Inter-gNB DAPS Handover with gNB-CU-UP Change
objective: Test the inter-gNB handover procedure involving Dual Active Protocol Stack (DAPS) handover with gNB-CU-UP change, focusing on data forwarding and status transfer.
preconditions: UE is attached and connected to source gNB-CU-CP and gNB-CU-UP with DAPS configured DRBs.
Source and target gNB-CU-CP and gNB-CU-UP are connected over F1 and Xn interfaces.
Bearer contexts are established with DAPS support.
steps: Source gNB-CU-CP sends HANDOVER REQUEST to target gNB-CU-CP.
Target gNB-CU-CP performs Bearer Context Setup procedure.
Target gNB-CU-CP sends HANDOVER REQUEST ACKNOWLEDGE with data forwarding info.
Source gNB-CU-CP performs F1 UE Context Modification procedure without stopping data transmission.
Bearer Context Modification procedure is performed to retrieve PDCP UL/DL status and exchange data forwarding info.
Source gNB-CU-CP sends EARLY STATUS TRANSFER message for DAPS DRBs to target gNB-CU-CP.
Target gNB-CU-CP performs Bearer Context Modification procedure providing COUNT info to target gNB-CU-UP.
Target gNB-CU-CP sends HANDOVER SUCCESS message to source gNB-CU-CP.
F1 UE Context Modification procedure is performed to indicate stop of data transmission.
Bearer Context Modification procedures are performed to stop packet delivery and retrieve PDCP UL/DL status.
SN STATUS TRANSFER message is sent from source to target gNB-CU-CP.
Bearer Context Modification procedure provides PDCP UL/DL status to target gNB-CU-UP if preservation is needed.
Path Switch procedure updates DL TNL address towards core network.
UE CONTEXT RELEASE and Bearer Context Release procedures are performed on source gNB.
expected_results: HANDOVER REQUEST and ACKNOWLEDGE messages are exchanged successfully.
EARLY STATUS TRANSFER message is sent and processed for DAPS DRBs.
Data transmission continues until explicitly stopped after HANDOVER SUCCESS.
PDCP UL/DL status is correctly exchanged and preserved as needed.
HANDOVER SUCCESS message is received by source gNB-CU-CP.
Data transmission stop is correctly indicated and executed.
SN STATUS TRANSFER message is sent and processed.
Path Switch updates DL TNL address correctly.
UE context and bearer contexts are released on source gNB.

title: Verify Change of gNB-CU-UP Trigger and Bearer Context Setup
objective: Ensure that the change of gNB-CU-UP is correctly triggered in gNB-CU-CP and Bearer Context Setup procedure is performed successfully.
preconditions: UE is registered and connected with active PDU sessions.
gNB-CU-CP and gNB-CU-UP (source) are operational.
Measurement report from UE triggers the change.
steps: Trigger change of gNB-CU-UP in gNB-CU-CP based on UE measurement report.
Verify that Bearer Context Setup Request is sent from gNB-CU-CP to target gNB-CU-UP.
Verify Bearer Context Setup Response is received from target gNB-CU-UP.
expected_results: Change of gNB-CU-UP is initiated in gNB-CU-CP.
Bearer Context Setup Request is correctly formatted and sent to target gNB-CU-UP.
Bearer Context Setup Response is received confirming setup success.

title: Validate F1 UE Context Modification for UL TNL Address Update
objective: Verify that F1 UE Context Modification procedure updates the UL TNL address information for F1-U in gNB-DU.
preconditions: Bearer Context Setup procedure completed successfully.
gNB-DU is connected and operational.
steps: Perform F1 UE Context Modification procedure to change UL TNL address for one or more bearers in gNB-DU.
Verify that gNB-DU acknowledges the UL TNL address update.
expected_results: F1 UE Context Modification message is sent with correct UL TNL address information.
gNB-DU updates UL TNL address and responds with success.

title: Test Bearer Context Modification Procedures for Data Forwarding and PDCP Status Exchange
objective: Ensure Bearer Context Modification procedures enable PDCP UL/DL status retrieval and data forwarding information exchange between source and target gNB-CU-UP.
preconditions: F1 UE Context Modification completed successfully.
Bearer Context Setup completed.
steps: Send Bearer Context Modification Request from gNB-CU-CP to source gNB-CU-UP to retrieve PDCP UL/DL status and exchange data forwarding info.
Receive Bearer Context Modification Response from source gNB-CU-UP.
Send Bearer Context Modification Request to target gNB-CU-UP.
Receive Bearer Context Modification Response from target gNB-CU-UP.
expected_results: Bearer Context Modification Request messages are correctly formatted and sent.
PDCP UL/DL status and data forwarding info are successfully exchanged.
Bearer Context Modification Responses confirm successful processing.

title: Verify Data Forwarding from Source to Target gNB-CU-UP
objective: Check that data forwarding is correctly performed from source gNB-CU-UP to target gNB-CU-UP during CU-UP change.
preconditions: Bearer Context Modification procedures completed successfully.
Source and target gNB-CU-UP are connected.
steps: Initiate data forwarding from source gNB-CU-UP to target gNB-CU-UP.
Monitor data packets forwarded and verify integrity and sequence.
Check for End Marker Packet signaling end of forwarding.
expected_results: Data forwarding occurs without loss or corruption.
End Marker Packet is received indicating completion of forwarding.

title: Validate PDU Session Resource Modify Indication Procedure
objective: Ensure that PDU Session Resource Modify Indication updates the DL TNL address information towards the core network.
preconditions: Data forwarding is ongoing or completed.
AMF and UPF are reachable and operational.
steps: Send PDU Session Resource Modify Indication from gNB-CU-CP to AMF/UPF.
Verify that DL TNL address information is updated in NG-U interface.
expected_results: PDU Session Resource Modify Indication message is correctly sent and acknowledged.
DL TNL address information is updated successfully in core network.

title: Test Bearer Context Release Procedure After CU-UP Change
objective: Verify that Bearer Context Release procedure is correctly performed by gNB-CU-CP after successful CU-UP change.
preconditions: PDU Session Resource Modify Indication completed successfully.
Data forwarding and bearer modifications are completed.
steps: Send Bearer Context Release Command from gNB-CU-CP to source gNB-CU-UP.
Receive Bearer Context Release Complete from source gNB-CU-UP.
expected_results: Bearer Context Release Command is correctly sent and received.
Bearer Context Release Complete confirms successful release of old bearer context.

title: Verify U2N Remote UE Direct-to-Indirect Path Switch without gNB-DU Change
objective: To validate the successful switching of U2N Remote UE from direct to indirect path via a target U2N Relay UE under the same gNB-DU without changing the gNB-DU.
preconditions: U2N Remote UE is in RRC_CONNECTED state with gNB-CU and gNB-DU.
Target U2N Relay UE is in RRC_CONNECTED state under the same gNB-DU.
Measurement configuration and reporting between U2N Remote UE and gNB-CU is functional.
PC5 interface capability is supported by U2N Remote UE and U2N Relay UE.
steps: Perform Uu measurement configuration and reporting between U2N Remote UE and gNB-CU to discover candidate U2N Relay UE(s).
gNB-CU decides to switch the U2N Remote UE to the target U2N Relay UE under the same gNB-DU.
If target U2N Relay UE is in RRC_CONNECTED state, gNB-CU sends RRCReconfiguration message to target U2N Relay UE to perform reconfiguration.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message to gNB-DU containing path switch configuration for U2N Remote UE.
gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message to gNB-CU.
gNB-CU sends DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message including RRCReconfiguration message to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfiguration message to U2N Remote UE.
U2N Remote UE stops UP and CP transmission over Uu interface upon receiving RRCReconfiguration message.
U2N Remote UE establishes PC5 connection with target U2N Relay UE.
U2N Remote UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-DU via target U2N Relay UE.
If target U2N Relay UE was in RRC_IDLE/INACTIVE state, verify RRC setup/resume procedure is triggered to enter RRC_CONNECTED state.
gNB-DU sends UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message including RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-CU.
expected_results: Measurement reports correctly received by gNB-CU with candidate U2N Relay UE(s) and Uu measurements.
gNB-CU successfully decides and initiates switch to target U2N Relay UE.
RRCReconfiguration message is correctly sent and received by target U2N Relay UE if in RRC_CONNECTED state.
UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST and RESPONSE messages are exchanged successfully between gNB-CU and gNB-DU.
DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER with RRCReconfiguration message is successfully delivered to U2N Remote UE.
U2N Remote UE stops Uu transmissions and establishes PC5 connection with target U2N Relay UE.
RRCReconfigurationComplete message is sent by U2N Remote UE and received by gNB-DU.
If target U2N Relay UE was idle/inactive, RRC setup/resume procedure is triggered and completed successfully.
UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER with RRCReconfigurationComplete message is successfully sent from gNB-DU to gNB-CU.
Data transmission continues seamlessly via the indirect path through the target U2N Relay UE without gNB-DU change.
category: F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP - Path Switch

title: Verify Path Switch Procedure when Target U2N Relay UE is in RRC_IDLE/INACTIVE State
objective: To verify the behavior of the path switch procedure when the target U2N Relay UE is in RRC_IDLE or RRC_INACTIVE state at the time of switching.
preconditions: U2N Remote UE is in RRC_CONNECTED state with gNB-CU and gNB-DU.
Target U2N Relay UE is in RRC_IDLE or RRC_INACTIVE state under the same gNB-DU.
Measurement configuration and reporting between U2N Remote UE and gNB-CU is functional.
steps: Perform Uu measurement configuration and reporting between U2N Remote UE and gNB-CU to discover candidate U2N Relay UE(s).
gNB-CU decides to switch the U2N Remote UE to the target U2N Relay UE under the same gNB-DU.
Skip RRCReconfiguration message to target U2N Relay UE as it is in RRC_IDLE/INACTIVE state.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message to gNB-DU containing path switch configuration for U2N Remote UE.
gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message to gNB-CU.
gNB-CU sends DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message including RRCReconfiguration message to gNB-DU.
gNB-DU forwards RRCReconfiguration message to U2N Remote UE.
U2N Remote UE stops UP and CP transmission over Uu interface upon receiving RRCReconfiguration message.
U2N Remote UE establishes PC5 connection with target U2N Relay UE.
U2N Remote UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-DU via target U2N Relay UE.
Reception of RRCReconfigurationComplete message triggers RRC setup/resume procedure for target U2N Relay UE to enter RRC_CONNECTED state.
gNB-DU sends UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message including RRCReconfigurationComplete message to gNB-CU.
expected_results: Measurement reports correctly received by gNB-CU with candidate U2N Relay UE(s) and Uu measurements.
gNB-CU successfully decides and initiates switch to target U2N Relay UE.
No RRCReconfiguration message sent to target U2N Relay UE prior to path switch.
UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST and RESPONSE messages are exchanged successfully between gNB-CU and gNB-DU.
DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER with RRCReconfiguration message is successfully delivered to U2N Remote UE.
U2N Remote UE stops Uu transmissions and establishes PC5 connection with target U2N Relay UE.
RRCReconfigurationComplete message is sent by U2N Remote UE and received by gNB-DU.
RRC setup/resume procedure is triggered and completed successfully for target U2N Relay UE to enter RRC_CONNECTED state.
UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER with RRCReconfigurationComplete message is successfully sent from gNB-DU to gNB-CU.
Data transmission continues seamlessly via the indirect path through the target U2N Relay UE after it transitions to RRC_CONNECTED state.
category: F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP - Path Switch

title: Inter-gNB-DU Conditional PSCell Change using MCG SRB without MN negotiation - Successful Procedure
objective: Verify the successful execution of inter-gNB-DU conditional PSCell change procedure using MCG SRB without MN negotiation during EN-DC operation.
preconditions: UE is connected to source gNB-DU under the same gNB-CU.
Only MCG SRB is available; MN configuration remains unchanged.
EN-DC operation is active.
Candidate gNB-DU is available and ready to accept UE context setup.
steps: UE sends ULInformationTransferMRDC containing NR RRC MeasurementReport to source gNB-DU.
Source gNB-DU forwards RRC TRANSFER message to gNB-CU.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU.
Source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST (including CG-ConfigInfo) to candidate gNB-DU for each candidate cell.
Candidate gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE including requested target cell ID.
gNB-CU sends SGNB MODIFICATION REQUIRED message with generated RRCReconfiguration to MeNB.
MeNB and UE perform RRC Connection Reconfiguration/Complete procedure.
MeNB sends SGNB MODIFICATION CONFIRM message to gNB-CU.
Execution condition for conditional PSCell change is fulfilled.
UE performs Random Access procedure at candidate gNB-DU.
Candidate gNB-DU sends Downlink Data Delivery Status and ACCESS SUCCESS messages to gNB-CU.
UE sends RRCReconfigurationComplete embedded in ULInformationTransferMRDC to MeNB; MeNB forwards RRC TRANSFER to gNB-CU.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU to stop data transmission.
Source gNB-DU sends Downlink Data Delivery Status to gNB-CU and forwards unsuccessfully transmitted downlink packets to target gNB-DU.
Source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE.
gNB-CU may initiate UE CONTEXT RELEASE procedure toward other candidate gNB-DUs if applicable.
Source gNB-DU sends UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND to UE.
UE responds with UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE.
expected_results: UE context is successfully setup at candidate gNB-DU with correct CG-ConfigInfo.
MeNB and UE complete RRC Connection Reconfiguration/Complete procedure successfully.
Candidate gNB-DU successfully performs Random Access procedure and becomes target gNB-DU.
Downlink and uplink data flows are correctly switched from source gNB-DU to target gNB-DU.
Source gNB-DU stops data transmission and forwards unsuccessfully transmitted packets to target gNB-DU.
UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION and RELEASE procedures complete without errors.
gNB-CU maintains consistent UE context across involved nodes.
Conditional PSCell change is completed without MN negotiation.

title: Inter-gNB-DU Conditional PSCell Change using MCG SRB without MN negotiation - UE Context Setup Failure at Candidate gNB-DU
objective: Verify system behavior when UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to candidate gNB-DU fails during inter-gNB-DU conditional PSCell change.
preconditions: UE is connected to source gNB-DU under the same gNB-CU.
Only MCG SRB is available; MN configuration remains unchanged.
EN-DC operation is active.
Candidate gNB-DU is unavailable or rejects UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST.
steps: UE sends ULInformationTransferMRDC containing NR RRC MeasurementReport to source gNB-DU.
Source gNB-DU forwards RRC TRANSFER message to gNB-CU.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU.
Source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to candidate gNB-DU.
Candidate gNB-DU rejects UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST or does not respond.
gNB-CU handles UE CONTEXT SETUP failure accordingly.
expected_results: Candidate gNB-DU rejects or does not respond to UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST.
gNB-CU detects failure and does not proceed with conditional PSCell change.
Source gNB-DU continues serving UE without interruption.
No RRC Reconfiguration or Random Access procedure is initiated at candidate gNB-DU.
System logs appropriate error or failure indication for troubleshooting.

title: Inter-gNB-DU Conditional PSCell Change using MCG SRB without MN negotiation - Random Access Failure at Candidate gNB-DU
objective: Verify system behavior when Random Access procedure fails at candidate gNB-DU during inter-gNB-DU conditional PSCell change.
preconditions: UE context is successfully setup at candidate gNB-DU.
MeNB and UE have completed RRC Connection Reconfiguration/Complete procedure.
Execution condition for conditional PSCell change is fulfilled.
Random Access procedure at candidate gNB-DU fails.
steps: UE performs Random Access procedure at candidate gNB-DU.
Random Access procedure fails (e.g., no response or contention failure).
Candidate gNB-DU does not send ACCESS SUCCESS message to gNB-CU.
gNB-CU handles Random Access failure accordingly.
expected_results: Random Access procedure at candidate gNB-DU fails.
Candidate gNB-DU does not send ACCESS SUCCESS message to gNB-CU.
gNB-CU does not initiate UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU to stop data transmission.
Source gNB-DU continues data transmission to UE.
UE remains connected to source gNB-DU without interruption.
System logs Random Access failure for analysis.

title: Inter-gNB-DU Conditional PSCell Change using MCG SRB without MN negotiation - UE Context Release Cancellation
objective: Verify that gNB-CU can cancel conditional PSCell change by initiating UE Context Release towards other candidate gNB-DUs.
preconditions: Multiple candidate gNB-DUs are available.
UE context setup has been initiated for multiple candidate gNB-DUs.
Conditional PSCell change procedure is in progress.
steps: gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to multiple candidate gNB-DUs.
One candidate gNB-DU successfully completes UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE and Random Access procedure.
gNB-CU initiates UE CONTEXT RELEASE procedure towards other candidate gNB-DUs to cancel conditional PSCell change.
Candidate gNB-DUs receive UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND and respond with UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMPLETE.
expected_results: gNB-CU successfully cancels conditional PSCell change for non-selected candidate gNB-DUs.
Candidate gNB-DUs release UE contexts upon receiving UE CONTEXT RELEASE COMMAND.
No data transmission or signaling conflicts occur due to cancellation.
System maintains consistent UE context state across all nodes.

title: Inter-gNB-DU Mobility Procedure Using MCG SRB in EN-DC
objective: Verify the correct execution of inter-gNB-DU mobility procedure when UE moves from source gNB-DU to target gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU using MCG SRB during EN-DC operation.
preconditions: UE is connected to source gNB-DU under the same gNB-CU in EN-DC mode.
Only MCG SRB is available for signaling.
UE is in connected state with active data bearers.
gNB-CU, source gNB-DU, target gNB-DU, and MeNB are operational and reachable.
steps: UE sends ULInformationTransferMRDC message containing NR RRC MeasurementReport to MeNB.
MeNB forwards RRC TRANSFER message to gNB-CU.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to source gNB-DU to query latest SCG configuration.
Source gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE including full configuration information.
gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST to target gNB-DU including CG ConfigInfo to create UE context and setup data bearers.
Target gNB-DU responds with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE to gNB-CU.
